mcqs.question: In a max heap, if the root is at index 0, what is the index of the right child of node at index 2?
mcqs.explanation: Right child formula: 2i + 2 = 2(2) + 2 = 6. Left child would be 2i+1 = 5.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of inserting an element into a Binary Heap of size n?
mcqs.explanation: Insert places element at end and performs sift-up. In worst case, element rises from leaf to root, traversing height = O(log n).

mcqs.question: Which of these is NOT a property of a Binary Heap?
mcqs.explanation: Binary Heap is a complete binary tree, not necessarily balanced in the AVL sense. Balance refers to height difference in subtrees, which is guaranteed by complete tree property but not called 'balanced'.

mcqs.question: In a min-heap with 15 elements, what is the maximum number of comparisons needed to extract the minimum?
mcqs.explanation: Height = ⌊log₂(15)⌋ = 3. But max comparisons = height = 3 for sift-down (actually 4 if counting comparisons at each level). Standard answer: ⌊log₂(n)⌋ = 3, but typically stated as O(log n).

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of heap sort (in-place implementation)?
mcqs.explanation: Heap sort can be implemented in-place using the same array for the heap, requiring only O(1) auxiliary space.

mcqs.question: After building a heap from an unsorted array of n elements, what is the time complexity?
mcqs.explanation: Building heap takes O(n) time. Although each heapify is O(log n), there are only n/2 nodes requiring heapify, and the work distribution makes total O(n).

mcqs.question: Which operation returns the element at the root without removing it?
mcqs.explanation: Peek operation simply returns arr[0] (the root/max or min element) without modifying the heap. Time: O(1).

mcqs.question: In a max-heap, which traversal will give elements in non-increasing order when extracting all elements?
mcqs.explanation: Repeatedly extracting max from a max-heap yields elements in descending order. This is the basis of heap sort.

mcqs.question: What is the index of the parent of a node at index i in a 1-indexed array representation?
mcqs.explanation: For 1-indexed: Parent(i) = i/2 (floor division). For 0-indexed: Parent(i) = (i-1)/2.

mcqs.question: A binary heap is used to implement a priority queue. What is the time complexity of enqueue operation?
mcqs.explanation: Enqueue in priority queue = Insert in heap = O(log n). Dequeue (extract-max/min) is also O(log n), peek is O(1).

mcqs.question: Which of these sorting algorithms has worst-case time complexity O(n log n)?
mcqs.explanation: Heap sort has O(n log n) for best, average, and worst cases. Quick sort has O(n²) worst case. Bubble and insertion sort are O(n²).

mcqs.question: In a min-heap, the children of a node must be:

options.text: Greater than or equal to the node
Less than or equal to the node

mcqs.explanation: In min-heap, parent ≤ children. So children are always greater than or equal to the parent. This ensures the smallest element is at the root.

mcqs.question: How many leaf nodes are there in a binary heap with n elements?
mcqs.explanation: In a complete binary tree, leaf nodes are at indices ⌊n/2⌋ to n-1 (0-indexed). Count = n - ⌊n/2⌋ = ⌈n/2⌉.

mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of binary heap over binary search tree for priority queue?
mcqs.explanation: Heap guarantees O(log n) for insert and extract, while BST may become unbalanced leading to O(n) worst case. Also heap uses array with O(n) space vs O(n) nodes for BST.

mcqs.question: The process of repeatedly removing the root and restoring heap property is called:
mcqs.explanation: Heap sort works by repeatedly extracting the max (for max-heap) or min (for min-heap) and placing it at the end, then restoring heap property. This is the heap sort algorithm.

mcqs.question: In a max-heap with 31 elements, what is the maximum number of levels?
mcqs.explanation: Complete binary tree with 31 nodes has height ⌊log₂(31)⌋ = 4 (0-indexed) or 5 levels (1-indexed root as level 1). Levels = ⌊log₂(n)⌋ + 1 = 4 + 1 = 5.

mcqs.question: What type of binary heap would you use to implement a min-priority queue (lowest value has highest priority)?
mcqs.explanation: Min-heap keeps minimum at root, so extract-min gives highest priority (lowest value). Max-heap would be used where highest value has priority.

mcqs.question: Which node in a heap cannot have children?
mcqs.explanation: Leaf nodes are at indices floor(n/2) to n-1. They have no children by definition. In heap, last level nodes are leaves but leaf nodes can also be on second-last level if the last level is partially filled.

mcqs.question: What is the result of performing heapify on a node that is larger than its children (in a max-heap)?

options.text: The node moves down the tree
The node moves up the tree

mcqs.explanation: Heapify is only needed when heap property is violated (node smaller than child in max-heap, or larger than child in min-heap). If no violation, node stays.

mcqs.question: When heap sort is performed on an array, in which order are elements placed in the sorted array?
mcqs.explanation: Heap sort using max-heap: repeatedly extract max and place at end. This builds sorted array from end to beginning in descending order. To get ascending, either use min-heap or reverse at end.
